description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, acceptance test, staging, reports, and production, using the variables AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY and RELEASE_BUCKET. In the build stage add an analyse-mutation job using the baasie/node-awscli image that runs sh ./ci/mutation-test.sh and stores the HTML mutation report in reports/mutation/html as an artifact, and a build job using the same image that runs sh ./ci/build.sh and archives the coverage/ directory. In the acceptance test stage add an Automated Acceptance Test TF job using the baasie/terraform-serenityjs-rest image that runs npm i --silent then npm run aat with before_script source ./ci/release-name.sh and source ./ci/deploy-to-env-tf.sh, after_script source ./ci/release-name.sh and sh ./ci/destroy-env-tf.sh, allows failure, and always publishes the Serenity BDD site at target/site/serenity/ as an artifact. In the staging stage add deploy-staging-cf and deploy-staging-tf jobs that run sh ./ci/deploy-to-env-cf.sh and sh ./ci/deploy-to-env-tf.sh respectively, use the appropriate images, set aws_stage variables, and target the cf-staging and tf-staging environments. In the production stage add manual deploy-production-cf and deploy-production-tf jobs with similar scripts, images, variables, and manual execution triggers. Finally, in the reports stage add a pages job that creates a .public directory, moves coverage, mutation HTML, and Serenity BDD results into it, then moves it to public/ and publishes public/ as a pages artifact with a 30‑day expiration.
